title: Péter Magyar sworn in as Hungary’s prime minister ending 16-year Orbán era
summary_full: Jubilation in Budapest as new leader invites people to ‘step through gate of regime change’The pro-European centre-right leader Péter Magyar has been sworn in as prime minister of Hungary, marking the official end to Viktor Orbán’s 16 years in power.Saturday’s ceremony – during which Magyar had invited people to join him to “write Hungarian history” together and “step through the gate of regime change” – comes a month after his opposition Tisza party won a landslide victory in parliamentary elections. Continue reading...
